segments.text: Then it came to pass, at the end of two full years, that Pharaoh had a dream; and behold, he stood by the river.
Suddenly there came up out of the river seven cows, fine looking and fat; and they fed in the meadow.
Then behold, seven other cows came up after them out of the river, ugly and gaunt, and stood by the other cows on the bank of the river.
And the ugly and gaunt cows ate up the seven fine looking and fat cows. So Pharaoh awoke.
Now it came to pass in the morning that his spirit was troubled, and he sent and called for all the magicians of Egypt and all its wise men.
And Pharaoh told them his dreams, but there was no one who could interpret them for Pharaoh.
Then the chief butler spoke to Pharaoh,
I remember my faults this day.
segments.speaker: Chief Butler

segments.text: And Pharaoh said to Joseph,
I have had a dream, and there is no one who can interpret it. But I have heard it said of you that you can understand a dream, to interpret it.
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: So Joseph answered Pharaoh, saying,
It is not in me; God will give Pharaoh an answer of peace.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: Then Joseph said to Pharaoh,
The dreams of Pharaoh are one; God has shown Pharaoh what He is about to do:
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: Now therefore, let Pharaoh select a discerning and wise man, and set him over the land of Egypt.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: Then that food shall be as a reserve for the land for the seven years of famine which shall be in the land of Egypt, that the land may not perish during the famine.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: And Pharaoh said to his servants,
Can we find such a one as this, a man in whom is the Spirit of God?
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph,
Inasmuch as God has shown you all this, there is no one as discerning and wise as you.
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: You shall be over my house, and all my people shall be ruled according to your word; only in regard to the throne will I be greater than you.
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: I am Pharaoh, and without your consent no man may lift his hand or foot in all the land of Egypt.
segments.speaker: Pharaoh

segments.text: Joseph called the name of the firstborn Manasseh:
For God has made me forget all my toil and all my father's house.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: And the name of the second he called Ephraim:
For God has caused me to be fruitful in the land of my affliction.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: Then Pharaoh said to all the Egyptians,
Go to Joseph; whatever he says to you, do.
segments.speaker: Pharaoh
